info.name: Bitmonie API — v1.2
info.description:
All endpoints grouped by module, plus simulated provider webhooks.

v1.2 makes loans **open-term**: no duration, no due date. Daily interest (0.3% on outstanding principal) accrues until repayment; the only forced-close path is an LTV breach (collateral coverage `< 1.10`). Customer-facing coverage nudges fire at `< 1.20` (WARN) and `< 1.15` (MARGIN_CALL). Custody fee is removed (pinned at 0). Auth is **passwordless** — email OTP only at login; TOTP + transaction-PIN are step-up factors, never login factors.

**End-to-end happy-path flow:**
1. Open → Variables tab → set `blink_webhook_secret`, `blink_account_id`, `blink_wallet_btc_id` to match `.env`. Defaults wired to the local `.env`.
2. `Auth → Signup` (email only — defaults to `bitmonie-smoke@example.com`).
3. Grab the OTP from the Mailgun inbox for `user_email`, paste it into `Auth → Verify Email` and run.
4. `Auth → Login (request OTP)` then `Auth → Login (verify OTP)` — the verify step captures the session token into `session_token` and rides as a cookie on subsequent requests.
5. `KYC → tier-1` — on success, the user's permanent NGN repayment VA is provisioned (read it out of the DB into `virtual_account_no` for the PalmPay webhook later). Loans ≤ ₦500,000 are KYC-exempt.
6. `Disbursement Accounts → add BANK` — name-matched against your User row first (KYC legal-name fallback).
7. `Loans → calculate` — daily rates only (open-term; no projections). Multiply daily interest by the days you intend to hold.
8. `Loans → checkout` — creates the loan + Lightning invoice; capture `loan_id` + `payment_hash`.
9. `Webhooks → blink (collateral confirmed)` — flips loan PENDING_COLLATERAL → ACTIVE, dispatches the NGN disbursement.
10. (After some days) `Webhooks → palmpay (collection — repayment received)` — auto-credits via the waterfall. Use the same VA number from step 5.

**Add-on flows:**
- `Loans → add-collateral` — fresh variable-amount Lightning invoice; mirror with the Blink webhook (use the new `topup_payment_hash`) to bump `collateral_amount_sat` while staying ACTIVE.
- `Inflows → unmatched` + `apply` — the "stack of cash rolls" self-serve surface when a customer has 2+ ACTIVE loans (auto-match impossible) or after an unmatched-inflow ops alert. `claim-inflow` on the loan is the deprecated predecessor.

**Collection pre-request hook** auto-injects `Idempotency-Key: <uuid>` on every POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE (skipped on webhook routes).

**Webhook folder** pre-request scripts compute valid Svix (Blink) signatures from `blink_webhook_secret` before sending.

request.description: Current buy/sell rates for SAT_NGN, BTC_NGN, USDT_NGN. Cached 10 min in Redis. Returns 422 PRICE_FEED_STALE if older than 10 minutes.

Public loan quote calculator (v1.2 — open-term, daily rates only, no projections).

Returns:
- **Daily rates:** `origination_fee_ngn` (waived = 0), `daily_interest_ngn` (`0.3% × principal`), `daily_custody_fee_ngn` (pinned at 0 for API stability), `daily_interest_rate_bps = 30`.
- **Collateral sizing:** `collateral_amount_sat`, `initial_collateral_usd` (sourced from Blink BTC/USD), `sat_ngn_rate_at_creation`.
- **UI thresholds** (move daily once accrual starts): `initial_liquidation_rate_ngn` (coverage 1.10), `initial_alert_rate_ngn` (coverage 1.20).

Loans are open-term — there is no duration to project against; the customer multiplies `daily_interest_ngn` by the days they intend to hold. Min loan ₦10,000 / max ₦10,000,000 self-serve.

Catalogue of NGN bank-transfer destinations the active disbursement provider can route to. Use the returned `code` as `provider_code` when adding a BANK disbursement account. Public (throttled 60/min/IP).
Public platform configuration the web client hydrates from: loan limits, LTV / coverage thresholds, daily interest rate, KYC-exemption threshold, supported currencies, etc.

Large-loan enquiry form (> ₦10M self-serve cap) for human white-glove follow-up.

- `collateral_type`: one of the display values (`Bitcoin (BTC)`, `USDT / USDC`, `MacBook (M1 or newer)`, …).
- `website`: honeypot — must be empty.
- `rendered_at` (optional): timestamp the form was rendered, for spam timing checks.

item.name: 1. Auth
item.description: Passwordless customer auth. Login = email OTP only (request-otp → verify-otp). TOTP is never consulted at login — it is a step-up factor only, alongside the transaction PIN.

request.description: Passwordless signup — the only field is `email`. A 6-digit verify OTP is sent by email (Mailgun). Names + KYC are collected later. There is no password column on the customer User.
Paste the 6-digit OTP from the signup email into `otp` before sending. Marks `email_verified`.
Re-sends the email-verification OTP.
Step 1 of passwordless login. Emails a `login`-purpose OTP. Always returns 200 — never leaks whether the account exists. Rate-limited.

Step 2 of passwordless login. Paste the `login` OTP from the email. Sets the `session` cookie + returns `{ message, token, expires_in }`. The test script captures `token` into the `session_token` collection variable (used as `Authorization: Bearer` for mobile clients).
Revokes the current session and clears the cookie.
Kills every active session for the current user.
Returns { otpauth_url, qr_code_uri }. Scan the QR with Google Authenticator / 1Password. TOTP is a step-up factor, not a login factor.
Confirms TOTP enrolment with a current 6-digit code.
Disables TOTP, verified with a current code.

item.name: Transaction PIN
item.description: Opt-in 6-digit numeric step-up factor (Argon2id). Every mutation requires an email OTP delivered to the user's inbox. Lockout after repeated wrong tries.

request.description: Emails the OTP needed to set a transaction PIN.
Sets the PIN. `transaction_pin` must be exactly 6 digits. `email_otp` from request-set-otp.
Emails the OTP needed to change the transaction PIN.
Changes the PIN. Requires email OTP plus the current PIN.
Emails the OTP needed to disable the transaction PIN.
Disables the PIN. Email OTP always required, PLUS proof: send EITHER `current_transaction_pin` OR `totp_code` (recovery path when the user forgot the PIN but has their authenticator).
Session-identity probe. Returns `transaction_pin_set: boolean` so the web client can nudge the user to configure a step-up factor.

item.name: 2. Users & Profile
item.description: Authenticated profile + home-screen aggregates. `/v1/users/me` is the full profile; `/v1/auth/me` is a thin session probe.

request.description: Returns the authenticated user's full profile: identity fields, country, KYC tier, TOTP state, account flags (is_active, disbursement_enabled, loan_enabled), and the permanent NGN repayment VA (null until tier-1 KYC provisions one).

Home-screen aggregate: `outstanding_ngn`, `daily_accrual_ngn`, `active_loan_count`, `attention[]` (coverage/action cards with urgency), and unmatched-inflow rollups (`unmatched_inflow_count`, `unmatched_inflow_total_ngn`).

query.description: Opaque cursor from the previous page's `next_cursor`. Omit on first page.

request.description: Cursor-paginated unified activity feed (loan events, repayments, collateral movements). Returns `{ items[], next_cursor }`.

Tier-1 identity verification. Rate-limited per user. Hits the KYC provider — a real BVN lookup costs money. On success the user's permanent NGN repayment VA is provisioned.

Loans ≤ ₦500,000 (KYC_EXEMPTION_THRESHOLD_NGN) are exempt and can be taken without tier-1.

- `id_type`: BVN | NIN | PASSPORT | DRIVERS_LICENSE
- `id_number`: BVN = 11 digits, NIN = 11 digits
- `date_of_birth`: YYYY-MM-DD
Returns 501 — liveness check deferred.
Returns 501 — deferred.
Current KYC tier + verification state for the authenticated user.
Lists the authenticated user's KYC verification attempts per tier. Encrypted/hashed columns (BVN/NIN) are never exposed.
Lists the user's payout destinations.

Max 5 per kind. For BANK and MOBILE_MONEY, the account-holder name is looked up via the provider and matched against the User row first, KYC legal-name fallback (threshold 0.85). Mismatch → 422.

`kind`: BANK | MOBILE_MONEY | CRYPTO_ADDRESS
`provider_name`: human name (e.g. "GTBank")
`provider_code`: NIBSS/bank code ("058" = GTB) — use `GET /v1/banks`
`account_unique`: account number | phone | crypto address
`account_unique_tag`: optional — memo/destination tag for crypto
`label`: optional friendly name
Name-matched like BANK.
CRYPTO_ADDRESS skips the name-match step. Optional `account_unique_tag` for destination-tag chains.
Marks the account as the default for its kind.
Cannot delete the sole default account.

Creates an open-term loan + Lightning invoice via Blink. Status begins PENDING_COLLATERAL. Test script captures `loan_id` and `payment_hash` (Lightning payment hash = Blink webhook matching key).

- `principal_ngn`: 10,000 – 10,000,000 (self-serve cap)
- `terms_accepted`: required boolean — must be true
- `disbursement_account_id`: optional, falls back to default
- `collateral_release_address`: optional Lightning address for collateral refund

No `duration_days` — loans are open-term (v1.2). Only one PENDING_COLLATERAL loan per user at a time (409 LOAN_PENDING_ALREADY_EXISTS otherwise).
Lists the authenticated user's loans.
Loan detail + status timeline + live outstanding (computed by AccrualService).
Cancels a PENDING_COLLATERAL loan. Fails if loan already ACTIVE or terminal.
Returns the permanent NGN VA + current outstanding the customer should transfer to repay this loan. Repayments are matched by amount-against-outstanding (no narration).

Sets/changes the Lightning address collateral is released to once the loan is REPAID.

- **First-set** (NULL → value): authenticated session is sufficient — send `collateral_release_address` only.
- **Change** (value → value): step-up required. First call request-change-otp, then submit `collateral_release_address` + `email_otp` PLUS exactly one of `transaction_pin` / `totp_code`. Example change body:
```
{
  "collateral_release_address": "new@blink.sv",
  "email_otp": "123456",
  "transaction_pin": "246810"
}
```
- After release: 409 CollateralAlreadyReleased.

Emails a 6-digit OTP scoped to (user, loan) for changing an already-set collateral release address. Single-use, 15-min expiry. Submit it as `email_otp` on the PATCH above (alongside a transaction PIN or TOTP code).

**Customer-initiated collateral top-up on an ACTIVE loan.**

Returns a fresh **variable-amount** Lightning invoice — the customer chooses how much SAT to send. 30-min expiry.

Response 201:
```
{
  "topup_id":          "uuid",
  "loan_id":           "uuid",
  "payment_request":   "lnbc...",
  "receiving_address": "<payment hash>",
  "expires_at":        "ISO-8601"
}
```

The test script captures `topup_id` + `topup_payment_hash` into collection variables — use the latter as `payment_hash` in the next Blink webhook simulation to mark the top-up RECEIVED.

**Errors:**
- 404 Loan not found (or not yours)
- 409 Loan not ACTIVE
- 409 ADD_COLLATERAL_ALREADY_PENDING — only one open top-up per loan at a time

**DEPRECATED** — prefer `Inflows → apply` (`POST /v1/inflows/:inflow_id/apply`), which lets the customer pick the exact inflow.

Still works: delegates to `applyInflowToLoan` using the most-recent qualifying unmatched NGN Inflow for the user (≥ ₦10,000, within 24h, unmatched), credited via the standard waterfall (`match_method='CUSTOMER_CLAIM'`).

**Errors:**
- 404 Loan not found / not yours
- 404 NO_UNMATCHED_INFLOW
- 409 Loan not ACTIVE

item.name: 6. Inflows
item.description: The "stack of cash rolls" self-serve surface. Customers list their unmatched NGN inflows (pending IOUs) and apply each to a chosen ACTIVE loan — no ops involvement.

request.description: Lists the customer's unmatched NGN inflows. Each row carries `status: 'CLAIMABLE' | 'BELOW_MINIMUM'`. Untrusted inflows (re-query disagreement, credit_failed, requery_unconfirmed) are gated out — those stay ops-only. The test script captures the first inflow's id into `inflow_id`.

Credits the chosen inflow against the chosen ACTIVE loan via the standard waterfall (`match_method='CUSTOMER_CLAIM'`). **Bypasses the ₦10,000 floor** — the customer is directing the apply themselves. Idempotency-Key required (auto-injected).

item.name: 7. Webhooks (simulated)
item.description:
Forge inbound provider webhooks with valid signatures for local testing.

- Blink: Svix-signed HMAC-SHA256; pre-request script computes the signature from `blink_webhook_secret`.
- PalmPay collection: omits the `sign` field — the provider's verifier returns true when absent. Same path is used by real PalmPay virtual account payments.
- PalmPay payout: must include a valid `sign` (RSA-SHA1 with merchant private key). Forging is non-trivial; in production the real provider sends these.

exec: // Build body from variables so accountId/walletId/paymentHash stay in sync.
// Svix sign: HMAC-SHA256 of `{msg_id}.{timestamp}.{raw_body}` with base64-decoded secret.
// Remove Idempotency-Key — webhook endpoint is anonymous and dedups on provider_reference.

request.description:
**Simulates a Blink lightning receive.**

Pre-request script builds the body from collection variables (`blink_account_id`, `blink_wallet_btc_id`, `payment_hash`), computes a valid Svix HMAC-SHA256 signature, and sets the `svix-id` / `svix-timestamp` / `svix-signature` headers.

Set `blink_settlement_amount` to override the default 21,000 sats. To confirm a top-up instead of the initial collateral, set `payment_hash` to the captured `topup_payment_hash` first.

On success (initial collateral): loan transitions PENDING_COLLATERAL → ACTIVE, a Disbursement is created, and an Outflow is dispatched via the active disbursement provider.

**Simulates a PalmPay virtual-account inbound transfer (repayment).**

- `orderAmount` is in **cents** — 5000000 = ₦50,000.00 (a partial repayment, above the ₦10,000 floor).
- `orderStatus`: **1 = SUCCESS** on the collection scheme (PALMPAY_COLLECTION_STATUS_SUCCESS). This DIVERGES from the payout scheme (where 2 = success).
- `virtualAccountNo` must match a `user_repayment_accounts.virtual_account_no` row. Set the `virtual_account_no` collection var first (auto-populated when KYC tier-1 succeeds in your local env, or set it manually from the DB).

**Matching flow** (no narration):
1. Reject if `orderStatus != 1`.
2. Resolve user from `virtualAccountNo` → no match → unmatched (`no_user_for_va`), ops paged.
3. Floor check: amount < ₦10,000 → unmatched (`below_floor`).
4. Find user's ACTIVE loans. Zero → unmatched. Exactly one → that loan. Multiple → smart-match by amount-equals-outstanding (±₦0.50; oldest wins ties); no match → unmatched (`multiple_active_loans`).
5. Independently re-query PalmPay's order-query API. Only credits if it confirms status=success AND amount + VA match. Otherwise: defer (transient) or page ops (`requery_mismatch`).
6. Re-query passes → auto-credit via the waterfall (custody=0 → interest → principal → overpay).

`sign` is deliberately omitted — the verifier returns true when absent (collection path only).

Response: plain-text `"success"` (PalmPay's required ack format).

**Simulates a PalmPay payout status update.**

`orderId` matches the regex `/:outflow:\d+$/` so the controller routes it to the payout branch (not collection).

- `orderStatus`: 1 = processing, 2 = success, 3 = failed
- `sign` must be a valid RSA-SHA1 signature over MD5-of-sorted-params using the merchant private key. Forging requires the `PALMPAY_PRIVATE_KEY` from `.env`.

Note: even on webhook receipt, the handler **re-queries PalmPay** to independently confirm the status before mutating outflow state — so a forged webhook alone cannot flip a payout to successful against a real PalmPay endpoint.

item.name: 8. Ops
item.description: Internal ops API. All routes require an `ops_session` cookie issued via `POST /v1/ops/auth/verify-2fa` (or `enrol-2fa` on first login). Customer `session` cookies are NOT accepted — OpsGuard reads `ops_session` exclusively.

request.description:
Step 1 of ops login. Provision the user first via `pnpm ops:create-user --email=... --full-name=...`.

Responses:
- `202 { challenge_id }` — credentials valid, 2FA enrolled. Pass `challenge_id` to `verify-2fa`.
- `403 OPS_2FA_ENROLMENT_REQUIRED { enrolment_token }` — first ever login. Use `enrolment_token` against `start-enrolment` then `enrol-2fa`.
- `401 OPS_INVALID_CREDENTIALS` — wrong email or password (deliberately ambiguous).
- `403 OPS_USER_DISABLED` — `is_active=false`.

Throttled 5/min per IP.

Step 2 of ops login. Redeem the `challenge_id` from `login` plus the current 6-digit TOTP. On success: sets `ops_session` cookie (HttpOnly, Secure, 8h fixed TTL) and returns `{ token, expires_in }`. Throttled 5/min per IP.
First-login 2FA enrolment, step 1. Exchange the `enrolment_token` from `login`'s 403 for a TOTP secret + QR. Scan it, then confirm with `enrol-2fa`.
First-login 2FA enrolment, step 2. Confirms the TOTP secret with a current code and mints the `ops_session`.
Returns the authenticated ops user's identity. Requires an `ops_session`.
Revokes the current `OpsSession` and clears the `ops_session` cookie.
Read-only — no audit row written. Returns each tier verification including the raw provider response. Encrypted/hashed columns are never exposed.

Idempotent retry of the post-KYC repayment-VA provisioner. Existing VA → returns the existing summary, no provider call, no audit row. New VA → creates the row and writes one `ops_audit_logs` (action: `kyc.provision_va`) entry in a single Prisma transaction. 404 if the target user has no tier-1 KYC.
Reset a user's KYC to tier 0. Atomic: deletes verifications, flips `user.kyc_tier`, and writes one `ops_audit_logs` (action: `kyc.reset`) row — all in a single Prisma transaction.
Revoke a user's KYC to a specific tier (0–3). Same atomicity guarantees as `reset` — writes one `ops_audit_logs` (action: `kyc.revoke`) row in the same transaction.

item.name: Ops Loans
item.description: Manual loan-lifecycle interventions for ops. Every action writes an `ops_audit_logs` row.

request.description: Reverses a LIQUIDATED loan back to ACTIVE when the liquidation was triggered by a bad rate (reason_code LIQUIDATION_REVERSED_BAD_RATE). Requires a `reason`. Writes the status-log + ops-audit rows.

Manually release collateral SAT for a REPAID loan when the auto path is wedged. Drives the same CollateralReleaseService as the post-commit hand-off and the safety-net worker; coordinated by a per-loan Redis SETNX lock. The `ops_audit_logs` row is written FIRST in its own transaction; the release attempt runs after. Refused unless the loan is REPAID, has a `collateral_release_address`, and is not already released (409). 502 if the provider rejects the send.

item.name: Ops Disbursements
item.description: Triage queue for disbursements. The default GET filter is `ON_HOLD` — disbursements whose latest outflow attempt failed and are waiting for an ops decision. `retry` dispatches a fresh Outflow attempt. `cancel` is terminal. `abandon-attempt` is the escape hatch for outflows stuck in PROCESSING. `recreate-for-loan` re-funds an ACTIVE loan whose disbursement was cancelled.

query.description: PENDING | PROCESSING | ON_HOLD | SUCCESSFUL | CANCELLED. Defaults to ON_HOLD — the active triage queue.
Disbursement.id from the previous page's `next_cursor`. Omit on first page.

request.description: Cursor-paginated list. Read-only — no audit row written. Each row carries `attempt_count` so ops can spot disbursements bouncing between attempts. Sorted by `on_hold_at DESC` so the most recently failed rows surface first.

Recreate a disbursement for an ACTIVE loan whose previous disbursement was terminally cancelled (or never funded the customer). Re-snapshots the loan's CURRENT default disbursement account and dispatches a fresh Disbursement + Outflow (202). Rejects when the loan isn't ACTIVE, a non-terminal disbursement already exists (409), or no default account / provider_code is set (422). Writes one `ops_audit_logs` (action: `disbursement.recreate`) row.

Disbursement detail with every Outflow attempt — per-attempt `provider`, `provider_reference`, `failure_reason`, `failure_code`, `initiated_at`, `confirmed_at`. Read-only — no audit row. Use this to confirm the latest attempt's status before retrying or cancelling.

Retry an ON_HOLD disbursement. Creates a new Outflow with `attempt_number + 1`, dispatches against whichever provider the router currently resolves, and writes one `ops_audit_logs` (action: `disbursement.retry`) row. Returns `202` — the new outflow runs asynchronously and resolves via webhook (or via the reconciler worker if the webhook is lost). 409 if the disbursement is not ON_HOLD.

Treat the in-flight outflow attempt (PENDING or PROCESSING) as failed and move the disbursement to ON_HOLD. Use when an attempt is genuinely stuck — stub provider in dev, or a real provider gone silent past the reconciler window. State transition reuses `OutflowsService.handleFailure` (with `failure_code=OPS_ABANDONED`). Writes one `ops_audit_logs` (action: `disbursement.abandon_attempt`) row. 409 if the disbursement is terminal or has no active outflow. Follow with `retry` or `cancel`.

Terminally cancel a disbursement. Captures `reason` + `cancelled_by_ops_user_id` on the row and writes one `ops_audit_logs` (action: `disbursement.cancel`) entry in the SAME Prisma transaction. After this, no further attempts. 409 if the disbursement is already SUCCESSFUL or CANCELLED.
